title: dodecatheon meadia var. jeffreyi
intro: Dodecatheon meadia var. jeffreyi, also known as Western shooting star or Jeffrey's shooting star, is a perennial plant species in the gentian family that is native to the western United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Dodecatheon meadia var. jeffreyi include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for treating various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or poultice, and its leaves and roots are often used in herbal remedies to promote healing and soothe irritated skin.
